title: The Lateral Ascent

features.name: pressure-marker
features.desc-on-touch: a single standing stone or post marking where the ridge flattens into ambiguity. you cannot tell if you climbed or descended to reach it. pressure and weather are one thing here.

features.name: lateral-wind-trace
features.desc-on-touch: the only mark that the wind makes here—not horizontal, not vertical, but perpendicular to all climbing. as if direction itself is confused about what it means to move.

features.name: ambiguous-passage
features.desc-on-touch: a gap in the stone that seems to move neither up nor down, but simply across. you pass through it and cannot remember which direction you were walking.

inscription: grammar fails here; direction becomes optional

file.name: Ridge_Lateral_Movement_Log_Pressure_Shifts_Direction_Undefined.txt
file.note: records of walkers who moved along the ridge without climbing or descending—their coordinates stayed level but their elevation record disagrees with itself. the archive files it as testimony to pressu

exits.label: back along the ridge to where pressure still has weight and direction
further lateral, where the ridge narrows and direction dissolves completely
exits.to: NEW: where the ridge becomes transparent and pressure becomes pure wind

exits.label: a path that might be up or might be a mirror of down, leading away
exits.to: NEW: the place reached by climbing that is nowhere higher

exits.label: another path that also might be climbing, might be descending, might be turning
exits.to: NEW: the path-that-goes-sideways where all directions are one
